title: To C. M.
content:
The lonely sunsets flare forlorn
    Down valleys dreadly desolate;
    The lordly mountains soar in scorn,
    As still as death, as stern as fate.

    The lonely sunsets flame and die;
    The giant valleys gulp the night;
    The monster mountains scrape the sky,
    Where eager stars are diamond-bright.

    So gaunt against the gibbous moon,
    Piercing the silence velvet-piled,
    A lone wolf howls his ancient rune,
    The fell arch-spirit of the Wild.

    O outcast land! O leper land!
    Let the lone wolf-cry all express -
    The hate insensate of thy hand,
    Thy heart's abysmal loneliness.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert William Service delivers a powerful performance in "To C. M."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
